observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:
require the Municipal GSO to submit the complete set of RPCPPE corresponding to all PPE classifications to the Audit Team to ensure reconciliation and validation of recorded balances;
direct the Municipal Accounting Office to revise the method of computing depreciation to comply with IPSAS 1 and 17, and to review and adjust the accumulated depreciation and carrying amounts to reflect accurate figures in the financial statements; and
require the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accounting Office to conduct regular reconciliation of their property and accounting records to ensure consistency, accuracy and completeness of information.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to: (a) record the necessary adjusting journal entries to reclassify the accounts noted; and (b) moving forward, ensure accurate and consistent recording of all transactions in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 and Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1 to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements as of year-end.

We recommended that the Management to facilitate fair presentation of accounts presented in the financial statements by:
instructing the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reclassify the items still recorded under CIP to their appropriate PPE accounts and to record the corresponding depreciation expense in accordance with applicable accounting policies; and
directing the Municipal Accounting Office, in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office, to regularly reconcile their records to ensure that completed projects are properly identified, recorded and reclassified in a timely manner.

We recommended that the Management:
instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to reclassify all items recorded under PPE, which does not meet the capitalization threshold, and make the necessary adjustments in accordance with COA Circular 2024-006; and
strengthen coordination between the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal GSO to regularly reconcile property records and ensure the accurate classification and timely recognition of semi-expendable properties in the appropriate asset or expense accounts.

We recommended that the Management:
instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to record the amount of repairs that met the capitalization criteria to the appropriate PPE accounts to present fairly the accounts presented in the financial statements, in accordance with IPSAS 1, 3 and 17; and
review all transactions related to repairs and maintenance to clearly distinguish between capitalizable expenditures and routine maintenance costs to ensure fair and accurate presentation of accounts in the financial statements

We recommended that the Management facilitate fair presentation of the accounts presented in the financial statements by instructing the:
MAO to conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of the breeding animals and their offspring, prepare a detailed status report, and furnish a copy to the Municipal Accounting Office as basis for appropriate recognition and valuation in the books;
Municipal Accounting Office to ensure the revaluation of Breeding Stocks every reporting period based on fair market value less cost to sell, taking into account all changes since initial recognition; and
Municipal Accounting Office to coordinate regularly with the MAO to reconcile reports and ensure that all biological transformations are properly accounted for and ensure that all required disclosures are accurately presented in the Notes to the Financial Statements, in compliance with IPSAS 27.

We further recommended that Management strengthen its monitoring and control mechanisms over the animal loan program by enforcing repayment terms and developing clear guidelines for evaluating and accepting offspring as repayment in pursuance with the MOA entered into between the Municipality and DA RFO 2 and in order to achieve the overall objective of the program.

We recommended that the Management identify and immediately address the causes of delay and ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are carefully planned and procurement and implementation ready, in order to maximize the utilization of the projects and to ultimately achieve the socio-economic developments and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management, through the MDRRMO and other concerned Offices, ensure that the PPAs for each budget year funded out of the 5% LDRRMF are well planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready to mitigate, if not totally prevent, delays in the implementation of projects to fully achieve the desired goals and objectives in reducing disaster risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management advise the BAC to ensure that the actual documents covered by its certification on the posting at conspicuous places are specifically indicated therein and to invite representatives from duly recognized private groups and NGOs, in addition to that from the COA, to sit as observers in the proceedings for the Municipality's various procurement projects in faithful adherence to applicable provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Management:
direct the Municipal Accounting Office to assign reference numbers to and indicate the date of preparation of DVs as prescribed under applicable provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
require all the approving authorities to strictly ensure that disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents are properly and completely accomplished as prescribed under Paragraphs 5 and 6 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management:
issue demand letters to the concerned Accountable Officers, directing them to immediately liquidate all outstanding cash advances with complete and appropriate supporting documents and to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare and record the necessary journal entries to recognize the related expenses in the books to facilitate fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and
strictly enforce the monitoring of the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances to ensure full compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing their use and to avert any risk of misappropriation or misuse of government funds.

We recommend that Management ensure strict compliance with the remittance of taxes, mandatory government deductions and contributions by, as much as practicable, promptly remitting intact all withheld funds and contributions during the month to the appropriate government agencies by the ensuing month, in faithful compliance with Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and other applicable laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer: (a) to coordinate with each other and review the discrepancy noted between their respective records, and (b) to regularly reconcile the check disbursement journal and report of checks issued at month end, all to ensure that check disbursements are recorded properly and facilitate fair presentation of cash accounts balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the following:
  
1. The LGU should comply with the requirements of the DPWH to comply with the general provisions of Executive Order No. 113 dated January, 1995.

2. Require the immediate submission of the copy of tax clearance by the owner of the 25,000 square meter lot located in Calamagui, Amulung, and for the Municipal Accountant to require complete documents before payment.

3. The Municipal Accountant should have a list of the lots that were recorded in the books for proper identification and to record the value of the real properties that were donated for proper recording in the books of accounts for fair presentation of the LGU’s properties.

4. The LGU to review the status of all the untitled lots covered by tax declaration whether these are existing and free from occupants for correction and for the Assessor’s office to prepare necessary documents for titling

5. The LGU should provide sufficient appropriation for transfer of title of ownership of title properties acquired through purchase/donations and the titling of untitled land acquired in favor of the local government if any.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts and other accounts that are affected in recording the RPT transactions and communicate to the Audit Team the result of the review of the said accounts. We also required the submission of the required financial reports to the Audit Team to give the team ample time to review the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks and require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant the original Disbursement Voucher (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV). We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to the Auditor the monthly BRS on time and if possible, secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of the stale checks in their possession for replacement.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balance of Due to LGUs account with the Provincial Accountant and to liquidate the outstanding fund transfer for the implementation of Early Childhood Care and Development. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer settle the unpaid procurement of accountable forms to the Provincial Government of Cagayan.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the supporting documents of the Journal Entry Voucher in setting up the Accounts Payable account at year-end. We also recommended that the LGU create a policy on the non-accumulation of reimbursement for travelling expenses for easy review and approval.

We recommended that the Management conduct actual physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality and prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be reconciled against accounting records, and submit a copy to the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Management notify and direct all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO), for the receipt and inspection of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department or end-user. The GSO shall likewise furnish the Accounting department the necessary records on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts. The Accounting Office shall make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to effect the corrections of unrecorded transactions pursuant to IPSAS 23.

We recommended that the Management immediately transfer the cash of all the unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for CYs 2017 and 2018 to the Trust Fund in compliance with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and for easy monitoring of the utilization and to ensure availability of this fund to support the activities of LDRRMC.

We recommended that the Management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. We also required the immediate submission of the CY 2018 Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Management comply with all categories of the minimum standards for disaster preparedness. We also recommended the formulation of a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, Department of Budget and Management and Department of Interior and Local Government, in close coordination with the local development council.

We recommend that the LGU fully comply with the staffing requirement in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and  stop employing job order personnel in performing mandatory functions to avoid possible investment loss in human resources.

We recommended that the LGU submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for the Team to evaluate its conformity with the approved plan and if directed towards the realization of our country’s commitment, plan, and policies on women empowerment, gender equality, and gender development.

We recommended that the Management comply with Sections 6.5.4.1 and 6.5.4.2 of the Policy Guidelines and Procedures in the Implementation of the FY 2018 Local Government Support Fund-Assistance to Municipalities (LGSF-AM) program to provide LGU constituents of their right to information on matters of public concern.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management look into the causes of low occupation rate of its market stalls by reviewing their policies/guidelines in the granting of market stall rights to its lessees to maximize revenue collection from market stalls operations.

We recommended that the Management maximize the use of the funds received as share from the tobacco excise tax, through proper planning and improved efficiency in its procurement process, so that the programs are implemented on time and the tobacco farmers obtain optimum benefit from said projects.

We recommended that the Management implement the projects as planned and address the causes of delays in the implementation of the eight (8) programmed developmental projects in order to maximize the utilization of the funds and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management implement projects as planned and address the causes of delays in the implementation of the 29 programmed developmental projects in order to maximize the utilization of the funds and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality. We further recommended that Management design strategies to immediately resolve the root cause of the increasing number of unimplemented projects.

We recommended that the Management review the balances of the continuing appropriations and the unexpended balances pertaining to prior years in the amount of P9,513,065.69 and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund at the end of the fiscal year.

We recommended to Management to submit to this office the Daily Time Records or Certificate of Service and Approved Application for Leave for the regular and casual employees. For the personnel under “job order” status please submit the DTRs and verified/accepted accomplishment report.

We also recommended to Management to comply with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 “Prescribing the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions” and to stop the practice of the disbursing officer paying salaries and other transactions under a payroll to persons other than the payees unless properly authorized by the payee.
